rulings.Case Title: JAMES ICARD et al VS. ECOLAB, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Of January 18 Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 19, 2011, line 1, DEFENDANT ECOLAB INC.'S Motion For Reconsideration Of January 18 Order. DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION IS DENIED. IT APPEARS THAT COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF MADE AN INACCURATE STATEMENT TO THE COURT, BUT IT DID NOT APPEAR TO BE INTENTIONALLY MISLEADING. FURTHER, IF THE MASSON CASE HAD NOTHING TO DO WITH THE HAZMAT EXEMPTION THEN NOTHING WILL NEED TO BE PROVIDED TO PLAINTIFF UNDER THE COURT'S ORDER. (SEE TRANSCRIPT P.15, LINES 20-22). REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS DENIED. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: EUREKA GARDEN HOMEOWNER'S ASSOCIATION VS. DONALD WILLIAM DANMEIER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 19, 2011, line 4, DEFENDANT MATTHEW KLUNIS MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Defendant Klunis¿ Motion for Summary Adjudication on the 1st and 2nd causes of action for breach of contract is granted because he is not a party to the contract. Further there is no evidence that defendant is a ¿partner¿ in the partnership or that there was a partnership formed under the Corporations Code. Defendant Klunis¿ Motion for Summary Adjudication on the 3rd cause of action for professional negligence is granted. The evidence shows that at all material times defendant was an employee of Danmeier Architects and not a licensed architect. Further plaintiff fails to produce evidence of physical damage thereby entitling defendant to protection under the agent immunity doctrine. Defendant Klunis¿ Motion for Summary Adjudication on the 4th cause of action for negligent misrepresentation is granted. There is no evidence that this defendant made the alleged misrepresentations, that defendant was acting, at all relevant times, solely as an employee of Daneier Architects, and because he did not cause physical damage to plaintiff, he is entitled to protection under the immunity doctrine. Defendant Klunis¿ Motion for Summary Adjudication on the 6th cause of action for ¿alter-ego¿ liability is granted. Plaintiff cannot establish both the requisite unity of interest between defendant and the alleged corporation and the requisite bad faith necessary to establish liability against him. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: ANDREA MANGAN VS. KEY MORTGAGE CORP DBA KMC MORTGAGE CORP
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 19, 2011, line 5, DEFENDANT GMAC MORTGAGE, LLC, ETS SERVICES, LLC DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. As to the 1st cause of action (Breach of Fiduciary Duty), the Demurrer is overruled. Defendant does not address the merits of its demurrer on this cause of action. As to the 2nd cause of action (Breach of Covenant of Good Faith and Fair Dealing), the Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff has sufficiently pled all requisite elements except that plaintiff did all or substantially all the things that were required of her under the terms of the contract. As to the 3rd, 6th, and 7th causes of action (Fraud and Deceit), the Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff allege with particularity facts that demonstrate how she could not have discovered the true nature of the fraud or misrepresentations through reasonable diligence (why the ¿later discovery¿ rule should apply.) Moreover, the claims are not stated with sufficient particularity as to who made an alleged misrepresentation, their authority to speak on behalf of the defendant(s), what the misrepresentation consisted of, and when it occurred. As to the 4th cause of action (UCL Claim), the Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff must plead with sufficient particularity which section of the UCL the defendant(s) violated and the acts that constitute the violation. As to the 5th cause of action (Promissory Estoppel), the Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend. This claim is contingent upon the 3rd, 6th and 7th causes of action being successfully pled. Plaintiff must allege facts as to why the reliance was justifiable. As to the 8th cause of action (Restitution for Unjust Enrichment), the Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. There is no cause of action for Unjust Enrichment; it is a form of restitution. As to the 9th cause of action (Quiet Title), the Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. This claim is barred by res
